name: DBI inflation
summary: Inflation driven by a D-brane moving in a warped throat of a string compactification. The brane scalar's kinetic term takes the Dirac-Born-Infeld form, imposing a speed limit on its motion analogous to the speed of light. Slow-roll inflation can occur on relatively steep potentials because the speed limit prevents fast rolling. Predicts large equilateral non-Gaussianity in the CMB, distinguishing it from canonical slow-roll inflation.

addresses_puzzles.note: String-theoretic UV completion of inflation; replaces 'flat-potential' fine-tuning with brane-scalar speed-limit dynamics. Predicts equilateral non-Gaussianity f_NL ~ -10 to -100 — Planck has constrained but not closed.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts equilateral non-Gaussianity at f_NL^equil ~ -10 to -100; Planck 2018 constrains f_NL^equil = -26 +/- 47 (1 sigma), allowing DBI in part of parameter space.
DBI inflation typically predicts very small r ~ 10^-5 - 10^-3, below LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 sensitivity in most realisations.

introduces.note: Throat warp factor, D-brane charge, potential, and speed-limit parameter.

history: Silverstein and Tong (2003), 'Scalar speed limits and cosmology: acceleration from D-cceleration', Phys. Rev. D 70, 103505, arXiv:hep-th/0310221. Alishahiha-Silverstein-Tong (2004) refined inflationary phenomenology. Active interface with string-cosmology + non-Gaussianity searches.

references.citation: Silverstein and Tong, 'Scalar speed limits and cosmology: acceleration from D-cceleration', Phys. Rev. D 70, 103505 (2004)
